span.p: Start: Logic Study Resources

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: Foundation Level

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Basic Logic Textbooks
Bergmann, Moor & Nelson: The Logic Book
Shoenfield: Mathematical Logic
van Dalen: Logic and Structure
Lemmon: Beginning Logic

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Introductory Resources
Tomassi: Logic
Smith: An Introduction to Formal Logic
Forbes: Modern Logic
Volpano: Introduction to Mathematical Logic

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Propositional Logic Study
Mendelson: Mathematical Logic (Ch 1-2)
Huth & Ryan: Logic in Computer Science (Ch 1-3)
Ben-Ari: Mathematical Logic for Computer Science
Rosen: Discrete Mathematics (Logic sections)

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Predicate Logic Study
Enderton: A Mathematical Introduction to Logic
Ebbinghaus, Flum & Thomas: Mathematical Logic
Crossley: What is Mathematical Logic?
Robbin: Mathematical Logic

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: Intermediate Level

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Set Theory Textbooks
Devlin: The Joy of Sets
Suppes: Axiomatic Set Theory
Jech: Set Theory (3rd edition)
Moschovakis: Notes on Set Theory

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Proof Theory Textbooks
Troelstra & Schwichtenberg: Basic Proof Theory
Girard: Proofs and Types
Pohlers: Proof Theory
Ranta: Type-Theoretical Grammar

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Model Theory Textbooks
Chang & Keisler: Model Theory
Hodges: A Shorter Model Theory
Tent & Ziegler: A Course in Model Theory
Poizat: A Course in Model Theory

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: Advanced Level

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Modal Logic Textbooks
Hughes & Cresswell: A New Introduction to Modal Logic
Goldblatt: Logics of Time and Computation
Fitting & Mendelsohn: First-Order Modal Logic
Garson: Modal Logic for Philosophers

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Non-Classical Logic Textbooks
Restall: An Introduction to Substructural Logics
Dunn & Hardegree: Algebraic Methods in Philosophical Logic
Priest: An Introduction to Non-Classical Logic
Urquhart: Many-Valued Logic

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Philosophical Logic Textbooks
Read: Thinking About Logic
Jacquette: Philosophy of Logic
Wolfram: Philosophical Logic
Gamut: Logic, Language and Meaning

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: Specialized Level

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Computational Logic Textbooks
Sterling & Shapiro: The Art of Prolog
Bratko: Prolog Programming for Artificial Intelligence
Fitting: First-Order Logic and Automated Theorem Proving
Robinson & Voronkov: Handbook of Automated Reasoning

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Category Theory Textbooks
Pierce: Basic Category Theory for Computer Scientists
Goldblatt: Topoi: The Categorial Analysis of Logic
Johnstone: Topos Theory
Lambek & Scott: Introduction to Higher Order Categorical Logic

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Applied Logic Textbooks
Partee, ter Meulen & Wall: Mathematical Methods in Linguistics
Kamp & Reyle: From Discourse to Logic
Blackburn & Bos: Representation and Inference for Natural Language
Russell & Norvig: Artificial Intelligence (Logic sections)

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: Practical Resources

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Workbooks & Exercise Collections
Bergmann: The Logic Book (exercises)
Bostock: Intermediate Logic
Jeffrey: Formal Logic: Its Scope and Limits
Kalish, Montague & Mar: Logic: Techniques of Formal Reasoning

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Software & Tools
Lean Theorem Prover
Coq Proof Assistant
Isabelle/HOL
Agda
Natural Deduction Proof Editors

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Online Learning Resources
MIT OpenCourseWare: Logic courses
Stanford Logic courses
Coursera: Logic specializations
edX: Formal Logic courses
YouTube: Logic lecture series

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: Alternative Entry Points

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Mathematics Students
Shoenfield: Mathematical Logic
Monk: Mathematical Logic
Hinman: Fundamentals of Mathematical Logic

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Philosophy Students
Hurley: A Concise Introduction to Logic
Copi & Cohen: Introduction to Logic
Bergmann, Moor & Nelson: The Logic Book

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Computer Science Students
Huth & Ryan: Logic in Computer Science
Ben-Ari: Mathematical Logic for Computer Science
Gallier: Logic for Computer Science

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Self-Study Recommendations
Start with one comprehensive textbook
Work through all exercises
Supplement with online resources
Practice with proof software
Join study groups or forums

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Research-Level Resources
Journal of Symbolic Logic
Annals of Pure and Applied Logic
Logic and Logical Philosophy
Notre Dame Journal of Formal Logic
Conference proceedings: LICS, CSL, TABLEAUX
